question: What is the default request timeout for the FastAPI HTTP client in version 0.104.0?

ground_truth.answer: The default request timeout is 60 seconds.

internal_docs.fastapi-internals-v0104:
FastAPI HTTP Client Internals (v0.104.0)

The FastAPI HTTP client uses httpx under the hood. By default, the request timeout is set to 60 seconds for all outgoing HTTP requests. This can be overridden by passing a timeout parameter to the client constructor:

  client = httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30.0)

Note: Setting timeout=None disables the timeout entirely, which is not recommended in production environments.

question: How many dimensions does the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 sentence-transformers model produce in its output embeddings?

internal_docs.sentence-transformers-models:
Sentence-Transformers Model Reference

Model: all-MiniLM-L6-v2
  - Architecture: MiniLM with 6 transformer layers
  - Embedding dimensions: 384
  - Max sequence length: 256 tokens
  - Training data: 1 billion sentence pairs
  - Use case: Semantic similarity, clustering, retrieval

Model: all-mpnet-base-v2
  - Architecture: MPNet base
  - Embedding dimensions: 768
  - Max sequence length: 384 tokens

question: What Docker base image is recommended for Python 3.11 slim production deployments according to the internal Docker policy?

ground_truth.answer: The recommended base image is python:3.11-slim for production deployments.

internal_docs.docker-policy-v2:
Internal Docker Policy v2.0

Approved Base Images for Production:
  - python:3.11-slim     (preferred — minimal attack surface, ~45MB)
  - python:3.11-alpine   (acceptable — even smaller but pip builds may fail)
  - ubuntu:22.04         (legacy only — not recommended for new services)

Not approved for production:
  - Any :latest tag
  - python:3.11          (includes build tools, bloated)

All production Dockerfiles must use a specific version tag. Using :latest is a policy violation.

question: In Pinecone serverless indexes, what is the maximum number of metadata fields per vector, and which cloud provider regions support the us-east-1 zone?

ground_truth.answer: Pinecone serverless indexes support up to 40 metadata fields per vector. The us-east-1 region is supported by AWS.

internal_docs.pinecone-internal-limits:
Pinecone Configuration Limits (Internal Reference)

Serverless Index Limits:
  - Max metadata fields per vector: 40
  - Max metadata value size: 512 bytes per field
  - Max vector dimensions: 20,000
  - Max index name length: 45 characters

Note: Regional availability depends on cloud provider. Check official docs for region lists.

results.title: Pinecone Serverless Regional Availability
results.snippet: Pinecone serverless indexes are available on AWS and GCP. On AWS, supported regions include us-east-1 (N. Virginia), us-west-2 (Oregon), and eu-west-1 (Ireland). GCP supports us-central1 and europe-west4.

question: What Python version is required by openenv-core>=0.1.0, and what pip install command should be used to install it in a Docker layer with no cache?

ground_truth.answer: openenv-core requires Python 3.10 or higher. The recommended pip install command with no cache is: pip install --no-cache-dir openenv-core>=0.1.0

internal_docs.openenv-internal-requirements:
OpenEnv Core — Internal Integration Notes

The openenv-core package must be installed in all environment server images.
Use the following pattern in Dockerfiles:

  RUN pip install --no-cache-dir openenv-core>=0.1.0

Note: The exact Python version constraint for openenv-core is defined on PyPI.

results.title: openenv-core · PyPI
results.snippet: openenv-core 0.1.0. Requires Python >=3.10. A framework for building OpenEnv-compatible reinforcement learning environments with FastAPI backends.

question: What is the default chunk overlap in the LangChain RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter, and what internal convention should be used for naming document chunk IDs in Pinecone?

ground_truth.answer: The default chunk overlap in RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter is 200 characters. Internally, document chunk IDs in Pinecone should follow the pattern: {doc_id}_chunk_{index}.

internal_docs.pinecone-internal-conventions:
Pinecone Index — Internal Naming Conventions

Chunk ID Format:
  All document chunks upserted to Pinecone must follow this pattern:
    {doc_id}_chunk_{index}
  Example: fastapi-docs_chunk_0, fastapi-docs_chunk_1

This ensures deterministic IDs and avoids duplication on re-indexing.

results.title: LangChain Text Splitters
results.snippet: RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter splits text by characters recursively. Default parameters: chunk_size=1000, chunk_overlap=200. The overlap helps maintain context between chunks.

question: What is the maximum payload size limit for Hugging Face Spaces API endpoints when using the Gradio HTTP interface in 2024?

ground_truth.answer: Hugging Face Spaces using Gradio has a maximum payload size of 50 MB for file uploads and API requests.

results.title: Hugging Face Spaces Overview
results.snippet: Hugging Face Spaces support Gradio and Streamlit apps. API endpoints via Gradio have a maximum payload size of 50 MB for file uploads. Larger payloads should use the Hub dataset API instead.

results.title: Gradio Blocks API Reference
results.snippet: Gradio's HTTP API accepts JSON and multipart form data. File size limits are enforced at the Space infrastructure level, not by Gradio itself.

question: What is the SLA uptime guarantee for Pinecone's Serverless plan as of Q1 2024, and how does it differ from the Standard plan?

ground_truth.answer: Pinecone Serverless offers 99.5% uptime SLA, while the Standard plan offers 99.9% uptime SLA.

results.title: Pinecone Pricing and SLA
results.snippet: Pinecone offers three plans: Serverless (99.5% uptime SLA), Standard (99.9% uptime SLA), and Enterprise (custom SLA). Serverless is billed per read/write unit. Standard includes dedicated pods.

results.title: Pinecone Serverless Launch
results.snippet: Pinecone's new serverless architecture eliminates pod management. Users pay only for queries and storage consumed, not idle capacity.

question: What is the rate limit for the OpenAI text-embedding-3-small model on the free tier (tokens per minute)?

ground_truth.answer: The OpenAI text-embedding-3-small model on the free tier has a rate limit of 1,000,000 tokens per minute (1M TPM).

results.title: OpenAI Rate Limits
results.snippet: Rate limits vary by model and tier. For text-embedding-3-small on the Free tier: 1,000,000 TPM (tokens per minute), 3,000 RPM (requests per minute). Paid tiers have higher limits.

results.title: text-embedding-3-small Model Card
results.snippet: text-embedding-3-small produces 1536-dimensional embeddings by default but supports dimension reduction via the dimensions parameter. Lower than ada-002 cost with better performance.
